attacking_style: Attacks on non-traditional terrain (flat sections of climbs, ridgelines, descents) rather than steep gradients. Prefers breakaway scenarios where energy conservation and tactical positioning matter more than pure climbing power.
team_tactics: Movistar deploys him in sequenced pacing roles on early climb kilometers before leaders attack, or releases him for stage hunting from breakaways when GC protection isn't priority. Minimal domestique support when attacking himself.
terrain_preference: Ridgelines, descents, and transitional terrain between climbs. Struggles on steep gradients (16%+) and extended final climbs where pure climbing watts determine outcomes. Excels when finishing involves tactical positioning over pure power.
vulnerability: Lacks final climb punch after bridging efforts. Steep gradient finishes (16%+) expose limited explosive climbing power. Without team support on flat approaches, cannot sustain gaps against organized chases.
key_patterns: Attacks on flat/descent sections rather than steep gradients (3 races: Romandie, Catalunya, Basque).
Refuses cooperation in breakaways to conserve energy for final effort (2 races: Basque, Dauphine implication).
Serves as early-climb pacing domestique in Movistar's sequenced attacks (2 races: Vuelta 2020, 2021).
Bridges to groups but struggles matching accelerations on steep final climbs (2 races: Vuelta 2021 stages).
Stage hunting prioritized when released from GC duties, showing versatility beyond domestique role (3 races).
form_trajectory: positive: First World Tour win at 29 in 2022 Dauphine suggests career-best form, holding off Roglič despite solo finale pressure. Most aggressive rider recognition confirms attacking confidence peak.
built_from: 2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 15
2021 La Vuelta ciclista a Espana Stage 7
